sanskrit: अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम्। परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम्॥
transliteration: avajānanti māṁ mūḍhā mānuṣīṁ tanum āśritam | paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūta-maheśvaram ||
literal_meaning: Fools deride Me, having taken on a human body; not knowing My supreme nature as the great Lord of all beings.
contextual_meaning: People dismiss the divine when it appears ordinary, because their perception is limited to surface form rather than essence.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between appearance and reality; defensive derision to protect limited self-concept.
trigger_scenario: Meeting a quiet coworker who later reveals they founded the company.
anchor_text: Look past the ordinary mask to recognize the extraordinary within.
